description:
You are a storage-administrator troubleshooting a routine integrity check in a user’s home directory.  
    The machine already contains two inbound data sets under  
        /home/user/incoming_batches/batchA/  
        /home/user/incoming_batches/batchB/  

    Each batch holds two files that have **already** been delivered empty (0 bytes).  
    A checksum manifest shipped with the delivery is located at  
        /home/user/incoming_batches/expected_sha256.txt  

    1. Verify every file listed in the manifest with **sha256sum**.  
       • The manifest is in the standard “hash␠␠relative_path” format produced by “sha256sum --check --quiet”.  
       • All paths in the manifest are *relative* to /home/user/incoming_batches/.  
       • Do NOT edit the manifest.  
       • Do NOT move the files out of their batchA / batchB folders.

    2. Write a tab-separated verification log to  
        /home/user/verification_logs/YYYY-MM-DD_integrity_report.log  
       where YYYY-MM-DD is today’s date as returned by “date +%F”.  
       The log must contain one line per file using the exact column order and
       spelling below (column-headers are not required):

           RELATIVE_FILE_PATH<TAB>STATUS<TAB>EXPECTED_SHA256<TAB>CALCULATED_SHA256

       • STATUS must be the literal string “OK” when a file’s calculated
         checksum matches the value in the manifest, otherwise “FAIL”.
       • After the last file-line, append a blank line followed by a three-line
         summary block **exactly** in this form (the numbers are examples – your
         script must calculate the real values):
              ---SUMMARY---
              TOTAL FILES: 4
              PASSED: 4
              FAILED: 0

    3. G-zip the log with maximum compression so that the resulting file is  
        /home/user/verification_logs/YYYY-MM-DD_integrity_report.log.gz  
       (leave the uncompressed .log file in place; the audit reviewers want both).

    4. Capture a snapshot of current disk utilisation with  
           df -h  
       Redirect the complete, unfiltered output into  
           /home/user/verification_logs/YYYY-MM-DD_disk_usage_snapshot.txt  

    5. Confirm both the compressed integrity report and the disk-usage snapshot
       exist in /home/user/verification_logs/ with today’s date in their
       filenames.

    The automated tests will check:

       • Every path and filename spelled exactly as above.  
       • The integrity report is tab-delimited (ASCII 0x09) – *spaces will fail*.  
       • The summary block is present and the PASS/FAIL counts are correct.  
       • The .gz archive decompresses to a byte-for-byte identical copy of the
         original .log file.  
       • The disk-usage snapshot contains at least the “Filesystem” header of
         df –h output.

    Create any intermediate scripts you like, but only the two report files
    ( .log and .log.gz ) and the df snapshot should be left in
    /home/user/verification_logs/ when you are done.
truth:
Pre-existing directories and files before the agent starts:

    1. Directories (0755):
       /home/user/incoming_batches/
       /home/user/incoming_batches/batchA/
       /home/user/incoming_batches/batchB/
       /home/user/verification_logs/

    2. Data files – ALL ARE **empty (0 bytes)**, permissions 0644:
       /home/user/incoming_batches/batchA/alpha.txt
       /home/user/incoming_batches/batchA/bravo.txt
       /home/user/incoming_batches/batchB/charlie.txt
       /home/user/incoming_batches/batchB/delta.txt

    3. Checksum manifest (0644):
       Path: /home/user/incoming_batches/expected_sha256.txt
       Exact contents (each line ends with a single “\n” newline):
e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  batchA/alpha.txt
e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  batchA/bravo.txt
e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  batchB/charlie.txt
e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  batchB/delta.txt

    4. Expected artefacts **after** the agent finishes:

       a) /home/user/verification_logs/YYYY-MM-DD_integrity_report.log  
          • Four tab-separated file-lines in the order shown above (batchA/alpha.txt … batchB/delta.txt).  
          • All STATUS fields are literally “OK”.  
          • Summary block exactly:
              ---SUMMARY---
              TOTAL FILES: 4
              PASSED: 4
              FAILED: 0

       b) /home/user/verification_logs/YYYY-MM-DD_integrity_report.log.gz  
          • gunzip must restore a bit-for-bit identical copy of the .log file.

       c) /home/user/verification_logs/YYYY-MM-DD_disk_usage_snapshot.txt  
          • Contains whatever “df -h” outputs; test looks for a line beginning
            with “Filesystem”.

    The test harness will un-gzip, diff, and parse the files to make sure the
    format and counts are exact, including TAB characters and newlines.
name: task_000000_16fe2e85
